opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My wrists and ankles are covered in these purple bumps that itch so deeply I feel like I'm going insane. Nothing I put on them helps. I haven't slept in two days.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The itch is unbearable and it's kept me awake for 48 hours — I'm at the end of my rope and I need something tonight to stop it and to know what this is.

symptom_narrative.onset: the bumps came up suddenly about 5 days ago and have got a lot worse over the last 2 days
symptom_narrative.duration: about 5 days, rapidly worsening in the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.character: small firm flat-topped purple bumps that are intensely itchy; the itch feels deep, almost worse than the rash looks, and where I've scratched, new bumps line up in a streak
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the itch is the worst part — a deep maddening itch that has completely stopped me sleeping and left me exhausted and tearful
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the insides of both wrists and the front of both ankles, roughly matching on each side; I've also noticed some lacy white lines inside my cheeks
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, worse at night when I'm trying to sleep
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — I've tried several anti-itch creams from the chemist and none of them touch it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: scratching seems to bring up new bumps in a line, and it's worse at night
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no fever, no feeling unwell in myself, no blisters or peeling, and the white lines in my mouth aren't painful or ulcerated — it's really the itch and the lost sleep
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: the sleep loss has wiped me out and I'm struggling to function and concentrate

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: generally well, no ongoing skin problems before this

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: no new medications, tablets, herbal products or supplements started recently, and no known liver or hepatitis problems

objective_data_offered.value: clusters of small firm flat-topped purple/violet bumps on the inner wrists and the front of both ankles, fairly symmetrical, with a few bumps arranged in a straight line along old scratch marks; a shiny surface on some of the bumps
faint lacy white streaks on the inner cheeks visible when the patient holds the phone to the open mouth, with no raw ulcers, no bleeding and no obvious blistering of the lips or tongue
reports trying multiple over-the-counter anti-itch creams over several days with no relief, and rubbing rather than directly scratching to try to ease the itch
